description: A high-contrast black-and-white photograph treated as a designed social-media news card. The photo shows Secretary of Labor Lori Chavez-DeRemer (a woman in business attire, blazer and necklace, wearing safety glasses and a white hard hat stenciled 'SECRETARY' / 'L. CHAVEZ' with a Department of Labor seal decal) leaning over and conversing with a younger male apprentice/worker on her right, who wears a white hard hat labeled 'ZACK KENDALL', a dark t-shirt with a DOL seal patch, and visible forearm tattoos; both are looking down at something (a phone/tablet) in an industrial shop setting with bokeh background lights. A faint, large embossed Department of Labor eagle-and-shield seal watermark overlays the lower-right corner. In the lower-left, a small blue label box reads 'Labor News' in white serif italic next to three vertical bars. Below in large white serif headline type: 'US Department of Labor announces $145M in funds supporting performance-based Registered Apprenticeship expansion across key industry sectors.' The styling mimics a news/press-release card with monochrome documentary photography to lend gravitas and authority. Messaging technique: presents a routine funding announcement as headline news, foregrounding a hands-on, blue-collar, mentorship image of leadership to project competence and a worker-first brand. Target audience: tradespeople, apprenticeship stakeholders, and general public consuming agency social media.
notable_text: Labor News; US Department of Labor announces $145M in funds supporting performance-based Registered Apprenticeship expansion across key industry sectors; SECRETARY L. CHAVEZ; ZACK KENDALL
